name: Project REDLIGHT
description: Project REDLIGHT is a purported top-secret project within the United States government, specifically the military-industrial complex, alleged to have been initiated to test and potentially replicate alien technology recovered from crashed unidentified flying objects (UFOs). The theory claims that highly skilled pilots and engineers were recruited to reverse-engineer these extraterrestrial vehicles, with the ultimate goal of developing advanced aerospace technology for military applications. Originating within UFO conspiracy circles, the claims gained traction with the emergence of alleged insider testimonies and leaked documents. Proponents often point to purported sightings of triangular UFOs, advanced aircraft with unconventional flight characteristics, and supposed connections to Area 51 as evidence. Mainstream perspectives dismiss Project REDLIGHT as a product of misinterpretation of classified military projects, technological advancements, and psychological phenomena. Skeptics argue that the evidence presented is largely circumstantial, lacks verifiable documentation, and relies heavily on anecdotal accounts and unsubstantiated claims. Furthermore, the sheer scale and complexity of reverse-engineering alien technology, along with the logistical challenges of maintaining such a secret project, are considered highly improbable by mainstream scientists and defense experts. The theory persists due to a combination of public fascination with UFOs, distrust of government secrecy, and the allure of advanced, potentially world-changing technology.
subtitle: Alleged alien technology reverse-engineering program

claims.claim: The US government recovered crashed alien spacecraft.
claims.evidence: Alleged leaked documents, eyewitness accounts of UFO crashes (e.g., Roswell), claims of recovered materials with unusual properties.
claims.criticism: Mainstream explanations for UFO sightings include misidentified aircraft, weather phenomena, and psychological factors. Roswell crash debris identified as weather balloon remnants. Lack of verifiable physical evidence.

claims.claim: Project REDLIGHT was established to reverse-engineer alien technology.
claims.evidence: Testimonies of alleged insiders claiming involvement in the project, reports of advanced aircraft with unconventional capabilities.
claims.criticism: Insider testimonies often lack corroboration and are difficult to verify. Advanced aircraft development explained by classified military research and development programs. Confirmation bias and misinterpretation of classified programs.

claims.claim: Reverse-engineered alien technology has been used to develop advanced military aircraft.
claims.evidence: Sightings of triangular UFOs (TR-3B), claims of anti-gravity propulsion systems, reports of aircraft with silent flight capabilities.
claims.criticism: Triangular UFO sightings attributed to conventional aircraft or drones. Anti-gravity propulsion remains theoretical. Silent flight achieved through advanced noise reduction technologies. Lack of publicly available evidence to support alien technology origin.

keyFigures.name: Bob Lazar
keyFigures.role: Claimed to have worked at Area 51 reverse-engineering alien spacecraft

keyFigures.name: Stanton Friedman
keyFigures.role: Ufologist who investigated and promoted claims related to UFOs and reverse engineering

keyFigures.name: Philip J. Klass
keyFigures.role: Debunker of UFO claims and critic of Project REDLIGHT theories

timeline.event: Bob Lazar's claims about working at Area 51 and reverse-engineering alien technology surface
Increased public awareness of Project REDLIGHT through documentaries, books, and online forums
